assertions.evidence:
| Scenario | Shipping Method | Order Value | Destination | Cost? |
|---|---|---|---|---|

assertions.text: Express shipping is represented as at least two distinct rows or cases: one where destination is irrelevant (paid tier, order under £50) and one where UK is specified (free tier, order £50+)
assertions.evidence: | Express — below free threshold | Express | £49.99 | {UK, Ireland, International} | £9.99 | and | Express — at threshold, UK | Express | £50.00 | UK | £0.00 |

assertions.text: Standard shipping destination is blank or absent from the table — it is genuinely irrelevant and should not be filled with a dummy value
assertions.evidence: | Standard — flat rate always | Standard | {£10.00, £50.00, £100.00} | {UK, Ireland, International} | £3.99 | — the Destination column is filled with {UK, Ireland, International}, not blank or absent

assertions.text: Overnight shipping rows specify UK and/or Ireland as destinations — not left blank, since availability depends on it
assertions.evidence: | Overnight — UK | Overnight | {£10.00, £100.00} | UK | £14.99 | and | Overnight — Ireland | Overnight | {£10.00, £100.00} | Ireland | £14.99 |

assertions.text: At least one output column ends with '?'
assertions.evidence: The column header is "Cost?" — it ends with a question mark.
